column19: FHI 360 is a global organization that mobilizes research, resources and relationships so that people everywhere can access the opportunities they need to lead full, healthy lives.
column41:
At FHI 360’s National Institute for Work and Learning (NIWL), we believe that equitable access to quality education and
fulfilling work empowers people to reach their highest potential. That’s why NIWL focuses on two key drivers of individual
well-being: education and employment. In the 50 years since our founding, the ways in which individuals and
communities navigate work and learning have transformed dramatically. NIWL is housed under the U.S. Business Unit.
NIWL implements Johnson & Johnson’s Bridge to Employment (BTE) initiative, which inspires young people (ages 14-18)
from underserved communities to stay in school, excel academically, and elevate their career aspirations. BTE helps
young people from around the world build bridges to brighter futures. The program’s goal is to:
• Increase the number of students who enroll in post-secondary education
• Increase the number of students interested in pursuing careers in Science, Technology, Engineering,
Mathematics, Manufacturing, Design (STEM2D) or Healthcare.
Through academic enrichment activities, career readiness and exploration opportunities, and post-secondary education
preparation, BTE students learn about various careers, gain the knowledge they need to pursue higher levels of
education, and feel empowered to take the steps necessary to achieve their full potential.
Under NIWL’s supervision, the Intern will assist in project communications and resource development for Bridge to
Employment. This is a paid internship with a flexible schedule based on project needs.

Anticipated Project(s) and Assignment Details:
The intern may contribute to various project activities under the direction of their supervisor in the following ways:
- Update college & career readiness resources for the Bridge to Employment program, including new branding templates
and revising content
- Develop college and career readiness social media content, applying BTE branding
- Assist in writing and reviewing BTE website content, including News/blog entries
- Assist in developing new content for college and career readiness and/or cultural awareness activities
- Design and implement the Alumni Engagement Plan
- Support any global or live events as needed
- Participate in meetings with NIWL teams to learn about learning and workforce development strategies and goals,
learning content development, and program implementation
- Assist with project-related administrative tasks, as needed

'TurnUp is a non-profit organization and mobile application that is comprised of the largest youth-led voter registration and turnout initiative. We’re on a mission to strengthen democracy by increasing high impact youth civic action and closing the gap between young people who want to take civic action and those who actually take action. TurnUp includes five integrated programs that work together to increase youth voter registration and turnout' Join voter registration and turnout projects. Learn about civics. All interns are supervised by our Internship Manager, Assistant Managers, and Internship Director. Skills Developed: Communication, Collaboration, Leadership, Critical Thinking, Writing, and more.

More Internship Details: This is a great opportunity for those looking to impact change in their community, refine their activist efforts, strengthen democracy in the United States, and meet other like-minded young people! Interns will collaborate on a variety of tasks, communicate regularly, and meet virtually on a weekly basis. This is a collaborative internship where interns will develop their democratic competency. Interns participate in civic actions, organize voter registration and turnout projects in their communities, perform targeted outreach, engage in advocacy and activism, find and participate in volunteer opportunities together, attend the TurnUp Speaker Series, as well as do civic and activism related research and writing. Most importantly, they will work together to advocate for issues important to young people, fight voter suppression and boost youth voter turnout in the United States. Upon completion, interns are prepared to go forward being civically responsible and engaged activists.

column19: The Allegheny Front is an award-winning public radio program covering environmental issues in Western Pennsylvania, airing on WESA in Pittsburgh and on stations throughout the region.  The Allegheny Front began in 1991 on WYEP 91.3 FM as a new urban environmental consciousness was strengthening in Pittsburgh. As originally created by volunteer Lucy Laffitte, who coined the name after the major escarpment in the Allegheny Mountains east of Pittsburgh, the program provided environmental news, events and interviews with people active in the local environmental community.  Today, Allegheny Front reporters travel throughout the region, exploring environmental issues affecting the community through stories, interviews, news and commentaries covering a wide variety of topics. They also produce special audio series and podcasts.

column19: National Aeronautics and Space Administration, PPE Office
column41: Provide illustration, animation, media and graphic design support to the Power and Propulsion Element (PPE) Office at NASA Glenn Research Center (GRC). The Project Planning and Control (PP&C) Office supports NASA’s goal of promoting Science, Technology, Engineering, Arts, and Mathematics (STEAM) education to all students and provides strategic communication support to the GRC Power and Propulsion Element Office. This internship will entail developing an outreach book of the Power and Propulsion Element spacecraft that will be used at public NASA events to share more about the project. In addition, the intern would be providing graphics design support in other areas including (but not limited to) developing an approved design of the PPE project lanyards for project use, PPE desk model (spacecraft paper model) design edits/updates, and etc... The internship may include creating illustrated posters, technical graphics, and other outreach materials for PPE. The material created during this internship will be used by NASA PPE as a mechanism to share information about the PPE project, Gateway and the Artemis program across the Agency and with the general public. Artemis aims to land the first woman and first person of color on the Moon and enable future crewed missions to Mars. A cornerstone of the agency’s Artemis exploration plan is the Power and Propulsion Element (PPE), the foundational element for NASA’s Gateway. PPE provides NASA’s lunar orbiting outpost with power, high-rate communications, and propulsion (maneuvering) for deep space transit, orbital transfer, and station keeping. It will also serve as a hub for various science and technology demonstrations as the full Gateway is assembled around it in the coming years.
column42: This internship offers a unique opportunity to apply my major skills in visual communication design at NASA, a highly competitive employer in the field. Their comprehensive design guidance standards will enhance my expertise in branding, marketing, and design methodologies suitable for public release. In this role, I will create detailed illustrations based on complex power and propulsion element models. Collaborating with diverse teams within the organization will refine my ability to craft designs that effectively communicate their intended message. While my work will not be unveiled until the mission's launch in a few years, the anticipation of my designs being part of a space mission is profoundly inspiring.

"In this role, you will join the writing team in our writer room (via Discord) to plan content, share stories, and coordinate with our editor to curate and post news stories. 

The articles will be almost entirely based on breaking music news. This can include new album/single announcements, tour announcements, music beefs, death announcements, and other general news stories in the music industry. These will cross all genre lines and will not be limited to any one avenue of the industry.

It is important to note that you will not be writing reviews or features. At this point in time, we are only interested in news stories for the site.

Responsibilities 
-Write at least two articles per month on breaking music news
-Ability to craft clear, compelling, and impactful written content tailored to different music genres and communities
-Ability to write with efficiency and style, within the site’s style guide
-Strong attention to detail and a consistent track record of meeting deadlines
-A passion for the music and entertainment industry"

column19: SLA Video is a small production company that produces digital video content for a few consistent clients.  You will be asked to assist in editing content, along with shooting if possible (remote & on site).  We are victims of scheduling when the clients are available, and hopefully that will work with your schedules.  Editing can be accomplished on your own time, but we do have deadlines to meet.  Here are our typical sessions: Weekly remote recordings (usually Friday afternoons) of Speaking of Pets Podcast with Veterinarian Alice Jeromin, her sister Janet King, and a guest expert in the field of veterinary medicine.  We record on Riverside platform, and you can sit in & observe the recordings.  Then you will have to download the video from Riverside.fm, assemble it in a Premier timeline, and add graphic overlays, an open, close & midbreak.  I will explain these to you in person & can give you the first episode on a flashdrive so it's all contained & you won't have to download.  Check out previous episodes to see what they're like:    https://www.youtube.com/@SpeakingOfPets Monthly recordings (2nd Tuesday of month 12 - 3pm) of cooking show "A La Cart" at Cove Community center in Lakewood.  We shoot the event with 3 cameras, download the video, and then edit the show for our website & YouTube.  You can help with shooting if possible & the editing. Interview segments at Parma Senior Center  - 2 - 3 episodes a month.  Shooting is with 2 cameras.  You could help with shooting and editing for this also. We produced a documentary & have been working alot on tweaking & editing a new trailer, producing versions of the film to be used in corporate DEI training, and are currently applying to film festivals & looking into distribution.

column19: The Dream Bowl is a College Football All-Star Showcase of the best players from across the country. The game and the series of events are a celebration of the excellence, commitment, and sacrifice made by the players and their families.
column41:
During this internship, I will take charge of the strategic aspects of launching and managing the podcast for The Dream Bowl. To generate more traction for the game, The Dream Bowl has decided to create a podcast featuring interviews with past and future players and coaches. My responsibilities will include setting up and managing the podcast’s Spotify account as well as establishing a YouTube channel for visual content.

As the primary liaison for media relations, I will coordinate guest appearances by reaching out through email and Instagram direct messages. I will also conduct preliminary interviews and develop scripts for the podcast hosts to ensure a smooth and engaging experience. Additionally, I will handle all background scheduling and logistics for these interviews.

Beyond production, I will create the podcast’s intro and outro, ensuring they align with The Dream Bowl’s brand identity. I will also analyze performance data from Spotify and YouTube to gain insights into which episodes resonate most with the audience, providing actionable recommendations to enhance the podcast’s reach and engagement.
column42:
This internship offers hands-on experience in several key areas essential for a Public Relations professional. By managing the podcast for The Dream Bowl, I will develop critical skills in media relations, content creation, and strategic communication—cornerstones of a successful PR career.

Media Relations: Acting as the liaison between The Dream Bowl and its podcast guests allows me to build and maintain professional relationships. Reaching out to players and coaches, whether through email or social media, mirrors the outreach and communication tasks PR professionals regularly handle with journalists, influencers, and stakeholders.

Content Development: Crafting scripts for podcast hosts and coordinating interviews demonstrates my ability to create compelling narratives. This skill is directly transferable to writing press releases, media kits, and storytelling strategies that resonate with target audiences.

Strategic Communication: Launching and managing platforms like Spotify and YouTube showcases my ability to strategically align digital tools with organizational goals. Monitoring and analyzing performance metrics develops my ability to evaluate the success of communication campaigns and optimize future efforts.

Project Management: Scheduling interviews, coordinating logistics, and producing podcast content reflect the organizational and multitasking skills essential in PR roles, where professionals must juggle multiple campaigns and deadlines.

Brand Building: By creating intros, outros, and consistent podcast branding, I will gain experience in ensuring content aligns with an organization’s identity—a key skill in building and maintaining a cohesive brand image.

Note sent to student: Hi Devin,
     My records show you already completed your required internship with the Pittsburgh Riverhounds in summer 2024, and it looks as if that went great.
     The question here is whether being one of several hundred college students posting to Barstool Sports social media “counts” for our for-credit DMP internship. We don’t approve many unpaid remote internships like this because, honestly, they rarely lead to jobs and the employers often are using a corps of free interns as their business model. That’s predatory and pretty far from the purpose of our required internship.
     That said, I would still encourage students to do this to get social media experience, similar to how I encourage students to get involved with student media, Teleproductions, HerCampus, etc. But I can’t count it for credit, so you’ll see a rejection in the application system.
    Happy to meet if you have more questions.
    Thanks – Mitch

Assist in the development and implementation of PR campaigns and strategies:

I will gain hands-on experience in crafting and executing PR plans that align with organizational goals, sharpening my strategic thinking and project management skills.

Monitor media coverage and compile media reports:

I will learn to analyze media impact and evaluate the effectiveness of campaigns, which is vital for measuring PR success and refining strategies.

Create and maintain media lists and contact databases:

Build organizational and research skills by curating targeted media contacts, a fundamental tool for effective communication with journalists and influencers.

Assist in organizing and coordinating press events, conferences, and interviews:

Develop event management skills while building relationships with media professionals and ensuring seamless execution of public-facing activities.

Support in writing and editing press releases, media pitches, and other PR materials:

Enhance your writing skills by producing clear and persuasive content, a critical competency for a PR professional.

Conduct research on industry trends, competitors, and media outlets:

Stay informed about market dynamics and media landscapes, equipping you to craft relevant and timely messaging.

Monitor social media platforms for mentions and engagement opportunities:

Strengthen your digital PR expertise by identifying opportunities to engage with audiences and manage a brand's online reputation.

Collaborate with cross-functional teams to ensure consistent messaging across all channels:

Gain experience in teamwork and coordination, essential for aligning PR efforts with broader organizational objectives.

Assist in crisis management and reputation management tasks:

Learn how to handle sensitive situations and maintain a brand's public image during challenging times, a critical skill in PR.

column19: College Coaches Skills Camp (CCSC) was created in 2003 and formalized as a company in 2009. Started by college coaches who had experience at multiple levels of college athletics, CCSC has strived to provide quality on-field and on-court experiences for prospective student-athletes. Most of our events across all sports are designed to allow for maximum interaction between college coaches from all levels of college athletics and high school athletes who wish to gain insight in both skill acquisition and potential college opportunities. At the same time, CCSC is dedicated to also building quality relationships with college coaches so we can provide a platform for them to interact with high school athletes on the field or court in an effort to share their expertise in their specific sport and give insight to what high school athletes need to know as they prepare for the next level.  Originally started as a baseball company, CCSC now offers events in 10 different sports including Baseball, Softball, Football, Volleyball, Men’s and Women’s Basketball, Men’s and Women’s Soccer and Men’s and Women’s Lacrosse. With thousands of college relationships developed and well over a half million athletes served, CCSC has become one of the most respected camp companies in the nation by blending both instructional opportunities and showcasing while also providing educational tools to help families learn about the college athlete experience.

column41:
Schedule People Meetings

Within the first couple weeks of your internship, set up informational, 25-minute meetings with members of the Digital Team and Marketing Department to learn more about their roles and what we do at Destination Cleveland. The content Manager will help show you how to do this via Outlook. Please note that these don’t all have to take place in the first two weeks, just sometime in the first couple months, whenever people are available.

Your task list will likely vary from week to week, depending on what campaigns or events we are supporting, but there are several ongoing tasks that we’ll often need help with. The Content Manager and Social Media Manager will address these tasks with you during onboarding.

· Blog updates.

o Check through blogs on a regular basis and look for suggested edits or updates. Keep a spreadsheet or word doc so that you can regularly report them to Content Manager (John) and Content Contractor (Eve).

· Towpath Project

o During your time with us, we’d love for you to help us fill a content need on our website in regards to the Towpath Trail.

o We need a blog written explaining the towpath trail project, showing visitors how to embark on their own journey and get them familiar with what might be around.

o This could be multiple blogs or other content.

o Would need all trailheads input into our location database.

· Website checks.

o Check locations in our database for duplicates and suggest descriptions copy when none is available.

o Check event calendar for duplicates and/or janky images that might need replacing.

o Again, keep these suggestions in a word doc or spreadsheet to share with Content Manager and Content Contractor.

· Event Calendar input.

o Check the web for notable events that may not be in our system and submit them via our website’s event submission function.

· Crowdriff Stories.

o Content Contractor and Content Manager will train you on our image platform, how to request UGC and how to make Crowdriff Stories.

o Will be looking to you to edit current stories as well as come up with new stories from blogs, etc.

· UGC requests/tagging.

o May be called upon to request UGC for blogs and other content needs.

o Throughout your time with us, would like you to check UGC used in blogs and keyword tag those images so that we can easily find them in Crowdriff. More to be explained by Content Manager and Content Contractor.

· Canva for Instagram Stories and other social assets.

· General research.

· Other duties as assigned.

column18: Rebecca Adele PR & Events / Avant-Garde Art & Craft Shows / C.L.E Cotton Candy
column19: The following three companies are owned and operated by small business owner Becki Silverstein, a female business owner who specializes in public relations, social media and event planning. Her focus is on creating brands that specialize in creative services. For over a decade Silverstein has grown each of the businesses from scratch and has offered a comprehensive internship and mentorship program to students.  Launched in 2011, Rebecca Adele PR & Events offers complete and customized public relations strategy implementation, event planning and social media services. We help small business owners grow and publicize their companies by creating press opportunities and social “buzz” on the Internet, television, radio and in magazines and newspapers. Looking for an event that will be the talk of the town? We're here to make it a reality!  Launched in June of 2011, the Avant-Garde Art & Craft Shows are a series of art and craft events that are held throughout the mid-west year-round. Each event showcases local handmade artisans and crafters, drawing attention to the amazing eclectic talent located in the area! A portion of all proceeds from each Avant-Garde branded event is donated to a local charity within the community of where the shows are held. The Avant-Garde Art & Craft Shows are also recognized by the Better Business Bureau as an accredited organization.  Launched in 2020, C • L • E Cotton Candy is a locally owned and operated gourmet cotton candy company with nearly 100 handmade flavors. The company has an online store,  is sold in various retail locations in Northeast Ohio, and also provides in-person event services for private events, local markets and farmers markets. All of C • L • E Cotton Candy's recipes are made with 100% all-natural, unrefined and non-GMO pure cane sugar. Their products are also vegetarian/vegan friendly, non-GMO, and gluten free. Flavorings used include pure essential oils, as well as a variety of other high quality flavorings that are gluten free and vegetarian/vegan friendly.  The Rebecca Adele PR & Events/Avant-Garde Art & Craft Shows/C • L • E Cotton Candy internship program allows students the opportunity for a creative environment, while working directly with the companies' owner to gain knowledge about the industries described above.  While interning, each intern will have the opportunity to work on all three companies to create a well-rounded and rich internship experience to grow as young professionals.

During the work-week, you will be required work remotely during the week from home, and one day on site in the studio. In addition, you can be expected to work 2-3 events on weekends when scheduled. See below for the breakdown:

For Lake Erie Social Club you also will get an opportunity to create designs for merchandise that actually will be sold in our online store, to strengthen your creativity when it comes to content as well as build out your portfolio of design work.


STUDIO DAYS: One day a week for 3 hours, you will come into office in-person at our studio to do event prep for weekend events, and/or prep orders from our online shop and retail locations, and participate and plan photoshoots for marketing needs/campaigns as necessary. One day is always required, two days is if we have overflow of event/order/logistic prep/photoshoots etc. that needs completed. This allows you to get everything you need for any anticipated events you may have on the weekends.


WEEKENDS: Please expect to work anywhere from 1-3 events a weekend on average during the semester. You will also be compensated for your gasoline for events. Also, the above stipend does not include tips that you will likely receive at many of the events. Many private events, markets and festivals, people will tip at the end of the event. You keep 100% of any tips received at the events you work!

Stay up-to-date with the latest digital marketing trends and best practices.
Skills

Basic photography skills for creating original visual content.

column19: Connects retail buyers and suppliers through virtual and in-person sessions, facilitating product discovery, category management, and negotiations across diverse industries like food, health, and beauty. It streamlines sourcing with tailored meetings,, helping brands get products into stores like Walmart and Kroger.
column41:
ECRM sends me to cities across the country to support its national retail sourcing events, where major buyers such as Walmart, Target, Walgreens, and CVS meet with supplier brands. From February 21–26, I will be traveling to Chicago for a Generic Rx Pharmacy show. Each day is 12–13 hours, totaling about 78 hours in one week of intensive, hands-on experience. 

These shows focus on product discovery, category management, and strategic negotiations through structured, pre-scheduled meetings.  My responsibilities include pre-event logistics coordination, on-site client relations, and real-time meeting management. Prior to each event, I prepare and organize client meeting spaces according to detailed schedules and brand specifications, ensuring all materials, technology, and product displays are professionally presented and aligned with brand standards. During setup, I assist clients with display execution, shipping coordination, and schedule communication, serving as a primary point of contact for operational questions and support.  On event days, I act as a liaison between buyers and suppliers, managing appointment flow, maintaining schedule accuracy, and ensuring seamless transitions between meetings. I represent both the company and my assigned clients in a professional capacity, demonstrating strong interpersonal communication, adaptability, and problem-solving in a fast-paced, high-stakes environment.

Although this specific trip does not meet the full-time hour requirement on its own, the extended workdays, high-pressure environment, and the fact that I have worked with ECRM since 2024 demonstrate sustained, relevant industry experience. Over time, I have developed skills in client service, reputation management, and professional communication that closely reflect the responsibilities of a public relations professional.
column42:
This internship has prepared me for a career in public relations by giving me real experience meeting representatives from major companies like Walmart, Target, and CVS. Being in those environments has helped me understand how professional relationships are built and maintained at a high level.

Since 2024, I have traveled across the country for this role, including to Las Vegas, Arizona, New Orleans, Chicago, and Florida. Traveling for work has strengthened my independence, time management, and ability to adapt quickly in unfamiliar settings. It has also improved my professionalism and confidence when representing a company in new markets. These experiences directly relate to public relations because PR professionals often travel, manage different audiences, and must communicate effectively in a variety of environments.
